title: Expand supported languages and enforce perfect alphabetical ordering across language selectors

requirements.summary: Expand built-in language constants to a comprehensive authoritative base-language set while preserving the existing 'none' option and browser-default fallback behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES contains a comprehensive list of base languages derived from an authoritative standard (e.g., ISO 639-1 base languages), using stable language codes and English labels.
Regional variants/dialects (e.g., en-US, pt-BR) are not included as separate entries; only base languages are included.
The existing option { code: 'none', label: 'No language (music)' } remains available in the list.
getBrowserDefaultLanguage() continues to return a supported base language code when possible, otherwise falls back to 'en'.

file_operations.description: Replace the current small language list with a comprehensive, authoritative base-language list (ISO 639-1 codes with English labels), keeping { code: 'none', label: 'No language (music)' } intact; ensure only base codes are present (no regional variants) and adjust getBrowserDefaultLanguage() to consistently map navigator.language to a supported base code (splitting on '-') with fallback to 'en'.

requirements.summary: Ensure languages display as one combined, strictly alphabetical (case-insensitive) list everywhere they are rendered, including built-in + custom languages, and maintain ordering after add/reload.
acceptanceCriteria: Both the floating language filter (frontend/src/components/layout/FloatingLanguageSelector.tsx) and the upload language selector (frontend/src/components/language/LanguageMultiSelectDropdown.tsx) render the language checklist in strictly alphabetical order by label (case-insensitive).
The ordering is applied to the combined list (built-in + custom languages) as a single list; custom languages are not grouped separately.
After adding a custom language, the list remains perfectly alphabetized without requiring a page refresh.
On reload, the list remains perfectly alphabetized after custom languages are loaded from localStorage.

file_operations.description: Centralize alphabetical sorting for the combined language list: derive a single combined array (built-in + custom) and return it sorted by English label using a strict case-insensitive comparator so that (a) adding a custom language re-sorts immediately and (b) loading from localStorage produces a sorted list on first render after hydration.

Ensure the rendered checklist uses the globally sorted combined language list from useCustomLanguages (and does not introduce any grouping); if any local transforms exist/are introduced, keep the final render order strictly case-insensitive alphabetical by label.

Ensure the rendered checklist uses the globally sorted combined language list from useCustomLanguages (and does not introduce any grouping); keep the final render order strictly case-insensitive alphabetical by label and stable immediately after adding custom languages and after reload.
